insight: Chieftain functions as efficient soak against freeze threats (like Endotherm Kit) even if not attacking with it, and blocking with it when frozen is generally correct play.
author: p0lari, tophwells
context: Player analysis of early game freeze defense strategy.

insight: Freezing a single Matrix with opponent's Endotherm Kit resources (Bite + Cryo Charge) for only 2 damage is inefficient; the chill threat of 3 lifespan is more valuable than converting to immediate damage.
author: p0lari
context: Discussion of when to use opponent's freeze resources vs. preserving chill threat.

insight: In sets with Thorium Dynamo, P2 can counter early double Dynamo with DD/1D opening to stay competitive in economy, avoiding an instaloss despite the eco boost.
author: .bky_1556, amalloy
context: Player asking how to punish AI's early double Dynamo opening.

insight: Thorium Dynamo is not playable when P1 can breach during the turn P2 has no tech and no income, or when 14-drone income is too slow for the set's tempo.
author: .bky_1556
context: Discussion of when Thorium is and is not a viable tech investment.

insight: Savior is viable when the set has good defense and P1 cannot punish a 3rd Engineer opening, and when no significant Red attacker (Big Red) appears before turn 9.
author: .bky_1556
context: Explanation of conditions under which Savior becomes a playable unit.

insight: Arms Race can be viable in a mutual-breach situation if the Omega Splitter is the best breach target, making it worth considering as a utility purchase.
author: .bky_1556
context: Brief comment on Arms Race viability in specific board states.

insight: Blastforge + Pixie costs 6B for 1 damage, which is worse than Steelsplitter's 6B for 2 damage, making Steelsplitter the superior attacker.
author: shadourow
context: Analysis of unit efficiency during game conclusion.

insight: Supply levels for units follow a pattern: 1-2 attack units typically have 10 supply, 3+ attack units typically have 4 supply, with exceptions for units with special abilities or defensive properties.
author: apooche
context: General discussion of unit supply balance philosophy.

insight: Grimbotch freeze decisions depend on absorber types: freezing 2LS Grimbotch with Vai is usually bad (loses tempo on offense), but can be correct if it forces opponent to sacrifice valuable defenders or blocks key unit interactions.
author: apooche, .bky_1556
context: Detailed analysis of freeze targeting strategy with Vai Mauronax.

insight: When freezing a Grimbotch with Vai to breach the opponent's best absorber, you can force them to choose suboptimal defense absorption (e.g., using Engineer instead of Borehole Patroller), gaining tempo advantage.
author: .bky_1556
context: Analysis of forcing opponent defense choices through freeze targeting.

insight: With Thermite Core, if you have an odd amount of damage, you can click one Pixie to make damage even and activate all cores without wasting any damage value.
author: .bky_1556
context: Pro tip for maximizing Thermite Core efficiency.

insight: Mobile Animus (Manimus) synergizes well with Valkyrion for single-Iceblade-per-turn strategies, providing flexibility to sell red units late game; it is less efficient than full Animus but enables tactical flexibility.
author: liadahlia, .bky_1556
context: Comparison of Mobile Animus and full Animus in specific set contexts.

insight: Smorcus is a highly efficient attacker, nearly as strong as old Flame Animus Perforator combo (which was nerfed), with Animus click yielding roughly Omega Splitter efficiency or slightly better.
author: .bky_1556
context: Analysis of Smorcus power level and efficiency comparisons.

insight: Smorcus + Animus click combo costs 11RR for 2 attack and is a lightweight combo purchasable over 2 turns, making it an aggressive early option.
author: .bky_1556, Deleted User
context: Discussion of Smorcus-Animus synergy efficiency and economy.

insight: 1RR click ability on Smorcus breaks no-click-cycles with Auride Core, preventing situations where opponent can farm resources without threat.
author: .bky_1556
context: Mechanical interaction of Smorcus click with Auride Core strategies.

insight: Shiver Yeti with Valkyrion functions like an infinite-stamina Rhino with +1 threat, making it an extremely valuable synergy for permanent attack pressure.
author: p0lari, darkeisbein
context: Analysis of Shiver Yeti utility with Valkyrion.

insight: Valkyrion + Cryo Ray is an auto-buy synergy when both are available; the 4G package cost for 2 Valk + 4 Cryo is efficient, with excess green easily converted to more Cryo.
author: liadahlia, kristian1103
context: Discussion of Valkyrion-Cryo Ray package efficiency and value.

insight: When holding all Valkyrions to avoid attacking (e.g., 4/0/4 pattern), you gain 3 free absorb denials while still defending the damage threat on the defense turn, making all-Valk rushes very efficient if you have no other permanent damage.
author: kristian1103, p0lari
context: Analysis of Valkyrion holding patterns and absorb value.

insight: Holding Valkyrions every other turn (vs. every turn) becomes less efficient as you accumulate more Valkyrions, since each additional Valk yields diminishing absorb denial value.
author: liadahlia
context: Analysis of diminishing returns from multiple Valkyrion holds.

insight: Mobile Animus costs 1 more gold than half an Animus, making it strictly less efficient for single Iceblade per turn compared to full Animus (which produces 2 Iceblades and can freeze a unit).
author: 307th
context: Developer analysis of Mobile Animus efficiency vs. traditional Animus.

insight: Mobile Animus + Iceblade can be good in fastblade strategies against Red-heavy sets where traditional Animus provides a bigger upgrade than the standard build.
author: redrame
context: Discussion of Mobile Animus situational utility in fast-blade strategies.

insight: Mahar Rectifier supply is high (10) because it functions as a primary attacker in situations where Animus is unavailable or undesirable, with 11GG/2 efficiency being acceptable for such scenarios.
author: .bky_1556
context: Explanation of high supply on situational attacker unit.

insight: P1 T1 Attacker opening in base + Smorcus may be difficult for P2 to refute, suggesting the Smorcus combo is particularly strong at early aggressive pressure.
author: .bky_1556
context: Analysis of early-game Smorcus pressure in base-set heavy games.
